name: Kivy (Cross-Platform Python UI Framework)

performance.notes: OpenGL ES 2.0 rendering; hardware-accelerated; mobile performance varies by device

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-touch Python UI for desktop and mobile. from kivy.app import App. from kivy.uix.button import Button. class MyApp(App): def build(self): return Button(text='Hello'). MyApp().run(). KV language for UI definition: BoxLayout: Button: text: 'Hi'. Touch input. Multi-touch gestures. Android/iOS deployment via Buildozer. Raspberry Pi support. OpenGL ES 2.0. Custom widgets. Animation system. Properties for reactive bindings. Steep learning curve vs web frameworks.

gotchas: KV language adds another syntax to learn alongside Python
Android deployment via Buildozer is complex and brittle
UI style feels dated vs native mobile apps
Development velocity has slowed vs Flutter for cross-platform UI
best_when: Multi-touch kiosk applications on Linux/Raspberry Pi
Cross-platform Python apps needing desktop + Android + iOS
When Python-native cross-platform UI is required
avoid_when: Mobile-first apps (Flutter has better mobile UX and tooling)
Desktop-only apps (Qt via PyQt6/PySide6 is more mature)
summary: Kivy is a cross-platform Python UI framework for desktop, Android, iOS, and Raspberry Pi. App + build() returns root widget. KV language for UI: BoxLayout: Button: text: 'Hi'. Multi-touch gestures. Buildozer for Android/iOS packaging. OpenGL ES 2.0 rendering. Property system for reactive bindings. Best for multi-touch kiosks and cross-platform Python apps. Steep KV language learning curve. For mobile-first use Flutter; for desktop use PyQt6/PySide6.
